title: Why run for the love of it when you could be making butter at the same time?

paragraphs.english: ‘Churning and burning’ is the latest trend in fitness – and all you need is trainers and a bag of cream. This could be just the start of a culinary CrossFit
paragraphs.pidgin: ‘Churning and burning’ na di latest trend for fitness – and all you need na trainers and a bag of cream. Dis fit be just di start of a culinary CrossFit

paragraphs.english: There is a new entry in the overstuffed “What fresh hell?” category for 2026 – people have started making butter while they run. “You may be asking yourself why,” content creator Libby Cope says in one of the videos that kicked off this weirdness (yes, yes I am); “The real question is why not?” She and her partner then pour cream and salt into double-bagged zip-lock sandwich bags, which they put into their backpacks, before setting off, “churning and burning”.
paragraphs.pidgin: e dey a new entry for di overstuffed “Wetin fresh hell?” category for 2026 – pipul don start making butter while dem run. “You may be asking yourself why,” content creator Libby Cope tok for one of di videos dat kicked comot from dis weirdness (yes, yes I dey); “Di real question na why no?” Im and im partner then pour cream and salt into double-bagged zip-lock sandwich bags, which dem put into dem backpacks, before setting comot from, “churning and burning”.

paragraphs.english: After a few miles, Cope opens her bag to reveal she has made a bit of butter. They have kept up this weird new hobby and, according to Runners World: “Now, the couple has more butter than they know what to do with.” (Do they know how much butter costs these days? Sell it!)
paragraphs.pidgin: After a small small miles, Cope opens im bag to reveal im get make a bit of butter. Dem get keep up dis weird new hobby and, as Runners World: “Now, di couple get more butter than dem know wetin to do wit.” (Do dem know how plenti butter costs dis ones days? Sell e!)

paragraphs.english: Others are copying Cope’s bizarre hack, which perhaps shouldn’t come as a surprise: butter is very now, unexpectedly rebranded as the perfect on-the-go snack (social media offers people chomping sticks of the stuff, or feeding it to babies). I can see this blowing up in the age of Make America Healthy Again, combining as it does three of the movement’s favourite things: animal fats, strenuous physical activity and being completely unhinged. Give it a few days and RFK Jr and Kid Rock will be running around in jeans and cowboy boots with papooses of unpasteurised cream strapped to their bare chests (something to look forward to).
paragraphs.pidgin: Others dey copye Cope’s bizarre hack, which maybe shouldn’t come as a surprise: butter na very now, unexpectedly rebranded as di perfect on-di-go snack (social media offers pipul chomping sticks of di stuff, or feeding e to babies). I fit see dis blowing up for di age of Make America Healthy Again, combining as e do three of di movement’s favourite things: animal fats, strenuous physical activity and being completely unhinged. Give e a small small days and RFK Jr and Kid Rock go be running around for jeans and cowboy boots wit papooses of unpasteurised cream strapped to dem bare chests (sometin to look forward to).

paragraphs.english: Although initially baffled by runners’ butter, I’m coming round. I see exercise as tedious but necessary, so the idea of harnessing it to something I enjoy more (eating) appeals. Surely there are other food-prep activities sufficiently strenuous they could double as a workout? I’m thinking I could devise a sort of culinary CrossFit: those 12-foot hand-pulled biang biang noodles are basically battle ropes you can eat; you could get a decent upper-body burn from kneading dough and hand whisking with enough volume and duration. And perhaps the cool-down phase could involve stamping some fruit into a restorative smoothie? The house will need a hose-down afterwards, but I reckon it’s a small sacrifice to Make Emma Dinner Again.

Surely e dey other food-prep activities sufficiently strenuous dem fit double as a workout? I’m thinking I fit devise a sort of culinary CrossFit: dem ones 12-foot hand-pulled biang biang noodles dey basically battle ropes you fit chop; you fit get a decent upper-body burn from kneading dough and hand whisking wit enough volume and duration. And maybe di ogbonge-down phase fit involve stamping some fruit into a restorative smoothie? Di house go need a hose-down afterwards, but I reckon e’s a small sacrifice to Make Emma Dinner Again.
